reasoning: Spain has edged ahead of France atop the prediction markets at roughly 17% to win, carrying Euro 2024 momentum, the highest floor of any contender, and a possession style that travels well in tournament football around Lamine Yamal and Pedri. France remains a co-favorite with the deepest forward line and Mbappé as a knockout-tie decider, but they were drawn into the Group of Death alongside a strong Senegal and Haaland's Norway, raising their early variance. The market is extremely tight with no runaway favorite—Portugal, England, Argentina, Brazil and a Curaçao-thrashing Germany all sit within striking distance—so confidence is genuinely Low this early in the group stage. Key injuries have reshaped the field (Brazil without Rodrygo and Estêvão, Netherlands without Timber/Simons, Spain losing Fermín López), while Morocco, a recent semifinalist who held Brazil to a draw, profiles as the most credible live outsider with a knockout-ready spine.
